title: 9 Standing Orders, Late Orders, and Contingency Orders
body_text:
9	Standing Orders, Late Orders, and Contingency Orders
9.1	Overview of Standing Orders, Late Orders, and Contingency Orders
Standing Orders, Late Orders, and Contingency Orders are submitted in ServiceNow. If ServiceNow is unavailable, see Section 9.6, Placing Orders via Fax or Email, for alternate procedures.
9.2	Logging in to ServiceNow
To log in to ServiceNow:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	YouГўВҖВҷll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itГўВҖВҷs your first login to the Client Centre, youГўВҖВҷve deleted your cookies, or youГўВҖВҷre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
ServiceNow
See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youГўВҖВҷve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under ГўВҖВңMy FavouritesГўВҖВқ.
Figure 3: Accessing ServiceNow
Select
Service Catalogue
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Select ГўВҖВңService CatalogueГўВҖВқ
9.3	Creating and Deleting Standing Orders in ServiceNow
The procedures in this section apply to all financial institutions. Allow three business days to process standing orders.
To create or delete a Standing Order:
Log in to ServiceNow as described in Section 9.2, Logging in to ServiceNow.
Select
New Currency and Coin Order System
in the Cash Ordering category. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Select ГўВҖВңNew Currency and Coin Order SystemГўВҖВқ
The userГўВҖВҷs contact information is pre-populated. Complete the rest of the form as described below. See Figure 6.
ГўВ‘
COS Location
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the name of the location or select the magnifying glass icon to choose from the list.
ГўВ‘ВЎ

and

are
automatically
populated based on the location selected.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Delivery Date
ГўВҖВ“
Select the date that indicates the first
delivery date
for the order.
Note:	If you are removing a standing order, this date indicates the delivery date on which you want to cancel a standing order (e.g., if the next delivery date is Aug 15 for a standing order that you want to remove, you would select Aug 15 as the delivery date).
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Order Type
ГўВҖВ“
Select
Standing Order
.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Select
Add
or

, as applicable.

ГўВҖВ“

ГўВ‘ВЈ

ГўВ‘ВЈ

ГўВҖВ“
Select a frequency for the new standing order (weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, or yearly).
ГўВ‘ВЁ
Delivery Day of the Week
ГўВҖВ“
Select the day of the week you want the new standing order delivered.
Note:	If you are removing a standing order, select the frequency and day of the week for the order you want to remove.
ГўВ‘В©
Comments
ГўВҖВ“
Add comments for any clarifications.
ГўВ‘ВӘ
Currency Order (product)
ГўВҖВ“
Select the denomination and quantity (enter the quantity in multiples of 100, except for $50 and $100 denominations, which can be ordered in multiples of 50).
ГўВ‘В«
Select the plus sign to add another row.
ГўВ‘В¬
Select
New Bank Notes
if you want new notes.
If you are ordering multiple denominations, but only want a specific denomination to be in new notes, make that specification in the Comments field. New bank notes are delivered on a best-efforts basis.
Note:	New bank notes are only available in Canadian dollar notes and must be submitted two business days before the dispatch date. Additional fees apply for new bank notes. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725, for fees. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
ГўВ‘Вӯ
Select
Submit
.
Figure 6: Example of Standing Order
The request is sent to Royal Bank, and they will set up or remove the standing order. Standing orders are displayed in Cash Services in the Pending & Ready tab along with other orders. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Example of Standing Orders in Pending & Ready Category
A standing order canГўВҖВҷt be edited in Cash Services, but a single occurrence can be deleted if necessary. The same deadline applies where an upcoming standing order would have to be deleted before 11:00 am, local time, one business day before the dispatch date. See Section 7.3, Managing Currency Orders in Cash Services.
When a standing order is deleted, only the selected occurrence is removed from Cash Services. The next occurrence of that standing order will be displayed in the Pending & Ready category with the information from the original standing order request.
Important
: Only one authorization is required for creating standing order requests.
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
9.4	Creating Late Orders in ServiceNow
Create a late order
in ServiceNow
if you require a cash delivery before the delivery date displayed in Cash Services. Late orders are processed on a best-efforts basis and are subject to additional fees (i.e., armoured car company fees and Central 1 fees). Your financial institution is responsible for arranging delivery of Late Orders with your armoured car company.
To create a late order in ServiceNow, you must first create a regular order in Cash Services. See Section 7.2, Creating Currency Orders in Cash Services. For example, if you miss the deadline for next-day delivery, enter the order in Cash Services for the earliest available delivery date the system allows. Submit the order and write down the confirmation number provided in Cash Services. This confirmation number is required in ServiceNow (see
ГўВ‘ВҘ
below) for Royal Bank processing.
Log in to ServiceNow as described in Section 9.2, Logging in to ServiceNow, and select
New Currency and Coin Order System
in the Cash Ordering category. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Select ГўВҖВңNew Currency and Coin Order SystemГўВҖВқ
The userГўВҖВҷs contact information is pre-populated. Complete the rest of the form as described below. See Figure 9.

Note:	New bank note orders are subject to additional fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725, for fees. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.

9.5	Creating Contingency Orders in ServiceNow
The contingency procedures in this section apply mainly to financial institutions in the north-western area of Ontario (i.e., certain locations in Thunder Bay) that receive cash from a different location than a Royal Bank Cash Services Centre. However, if the Cash Services application is unavailable, these procedures apply to all financial institutions.
Contingency orders are processed on a best-efforts basis. Submit the contingency order to Central 1 by 11:00 am local time, one business day before the dispatch date. Additional charges may apply depending on the nature of the contingency situation. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager. Your financial institution does not need to make special delivery arrangements since the order will be shipped as usual.
To create a contingency order in ServiceNow:
Log in to ServiceNow as described in Section 9.2, Logging in to ServiceNow.
Select
New Currency and Coin Order System
in the Cash Ordering category. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Select ГўВҖВңNew Currency and Coin Order SystemГўВҖВқ
The userГўВҖВҷs contact information is pre-populated. Complete the rest of the form as described below. See Figure 11.

New bank notes are delivered on a best-efforts basis.
Note:	New bank notes are only available in Canadian dollar notes and must be submitted two business days before the dispatch date. Additional fees apply for new bank notes. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725, for fees. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.

9.6	Placing Orders via Fax or Email
If ServiceNow is unavailable, Central 1 accepts Late Orders, new bank note orders, and Contingency Orders via fax or email.
Note:	If ServiceNow is unavailable and you urgently need to submit a Standing Order, contact Central 1, AFT Desk. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources, for details.
To fax or email Late Orders, new bank note orders, and contingency orders to Central 1 if ServiceNow is unavailable:
Complete the Currency and Coin Order, Form 1180. Completion of this form is self-explanatory. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Currency and Coin Order, Form 1180
Have the form signed by a signing officer on file with Central 1.
Fax the form to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs AFT Desk as directed on the form.
Contingency orders are processed on a best-efforts basis. Ensure that the form is sent by 10:00 am PT / 1:00 pm ET, one business day before the dispatch date for late orders and contingency orders, and two business days before the dispatch date for new bank note orders.
Related Information
Currency and Coin Order,
Form 1180
END
